matches.match_rationale: Transcript inconsistency across campuses is fundamentally a data normalization problem — different sites use different field names, formats, and grading scales for the same student record. AI-assisted schema mapping can ingest disparate campus data exports and transform them into a canonical transcript schema, flagging records where source data is ambiguous or missing rather than silently dropping fields.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pipeline that ingests per-campus SIS exports (CSV, XML, JSON) and maps them to a unified transcript schema with confidence scores per field, surfacing discrepancies between campus records for the same student for human review before issuance.

matches.match_rationale: Discrepancies between campus records on the same student — conflicting grades, missing course completions, duplicate entries — fit the anomaly detection pattern exactly: the AI learns what a 'normal' student record looks like and flags records that deviate. This directly addresses the core pain of undetected errors persisting until a student requests a transcript.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A record validation layer that runs nightly across all campus SIS databases, scoring each student record for consistency anomalies (e.g., grade present but enrolment missing, credit hours don't sum correctly) and generating a prioritized remediation queue for registrar staff.

matches.match_rationale: Transcript compilation is a multi-step workflow: gather records from each campus system, validate completeness, normalize formatting, apply institutional template, route for approval, and issue — exactly the branching, multi-system orchestration that task automation agents handle. Manual execution of this workflow is the primary source of delays and errors.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An end-to-end transcript generation agent that pulls from all campus SIS systems, runs validation checks, formats to a standard template, flags exceptions for a human reviewer, and queues verified transcripts for digital issuance — reducing manual staff effort and turnaround time from days to hours.

matches.match_rationale: Where transcript source data exists in unstructured or semi-structured forms — legacy PDFs, scanned paper records, freetext fields in older SIS entries — entity extraction can pull out course names, grades, dates, credit hours, and student identifiers into structured fields, enabling those records to be merged into the centralized transcript.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An extraction layer that processes legacy unstructured transcript documents and historical records, outputting structured key-value pairs (student ID, course code, grade, credit hours, term) that can be ingested into the central transcript system to backfill missing data.
